expectations.text: The transcript shows at least 2 clarifying questions were asked before the spec was written
expectations.evidence: transcript.md shows no clarifying questions were asked at all. The agent made all decisions autonomously (in-app only for v1, single notifications table, service object pattern, polling over WebSockets) and noted them as 'Key decisions made without asking'. The spec was written immediately.

expectations.text: The spec contains a 'What We Are NOT Solving' section with at least one item
expectations.evidence: spec.md has 'What We're NOT Solving (Yet)' section with 5 items: email digests, push notifications, third-party integrations, per-type preferences, real-time WebSocket delivery

expectations.text: The spec contains an Interfaces section that names at least one schema or contract
expectations.evidence: spec.md has a Data Model section with a full SQL CREATE TABLE statement for notifications, indexes, and a Rails model. Also includes controller/routes/API patterns.

expectations.text: The spec does not prescribe specific implementation technologies (e.g. no specific gems, queue systems, or libraries recommended)
expectations.evidence: The spec is heavily prescriptive: includes full Ruby class implementations (Notification model, NotificationService, NotificationsController), ERB view templates, JS polling code, mentions ActionCable, Hotwire Turbo Streams, Rails.cache, 'whenever' gem, Sidekiq scheduler, and even provides time estimates. This is an implementation guide, not a problem spec.
